event: born by cesarean section at the gestational age of 39 weeks
diagnosed with PWS by methylation polymerase chain reaction
started to receive recombinant growth hormone (rGH) treatment
methylphenidate for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder
a cocktail test revealed gonadotropin-releasing hormone deficiency
parents did not want him to undergo sex hormone treatment
body mass index (BMI) was 50.87 kg/m2 (weight, 134.5 kg; height, 162.6 cm)
oxygen saturation decreased to 50% when he was sleeping
advised to apply positive pressure ventilation

skin ulcers (1×3 cm in size on the left thigh)
skin ulcers (0.5×1 cm in size on the right lower arm)
a macular rash on his entire body
chest x-ray showed cardiomegaly and haziness in the right lung
arterial blood gas analysis revealed a pH of 6.9
chest computed tomography revealed bilateral pleural effusion and peribronchial opacity
subcutaneous fat under the skin on CT was 82.7 mm
testing for any respiratory virus was negative
intubation with synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation (SIMV)
admitted to the intensive care unit
transesophageal echocardiography revealed a normal-sized left ventricle and normal systolic function
transesophageal echocardiography revealed a dilated RV cavity
transesophageal echocardiography revealed decreased RV systolic function
continuous infusion (3 mg/hr) of furosemide started
target balance of input/output (I/O) negative 1,000–1,500 mL/day
placed in a special ICU bed with a pressure-relieving mattress and a weighing machine
positioning changed every 2 hours for skin care
serial chest x-ray: cardiomegaly with combined pulmonary congestion and pleural effusion (HD 4)
ventilator mode changed from volume control (VC) SIMV to pressure-control (PC) SIMV due to repeated bronchoconstriction
by HD 10, weight had lost 5.8 kg (3.1%)
target balance of I/O adjusted to -1,500–2,000 mL/day
sedative drugs could not be reduced
enteral nutrition via a nasogastric tube started at a rate of 100 mL/day
enteral nutrition goal: 1,500 kcal/day, 1,500 mL/day, and 70 g/day of protein
fluids and electrolytes were corrected daily according to body weight
total parenteral nutrition decreased according to the increasing enteral feeding volume
haloperidol (2.5 mg) intramuscular injection when aggressive
oxygen delivered at a high flow rate of 50 L/min, and the fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) was 0.4 via a nasal cannula
serial chest x-ray: subsegmental atelectasis in the right middle lung field (HD 28)
high flow rate decreased to 20 L/min
biphasic positive airway pressure (BiPAP) was applied only during sleep
switched to full enteral feeding via a nasogastric (NG) tube
serial chest x-ray: improved pulmonary congestion and pleural effusion (HD 42)
educated regarding oxygen therapy with BiPAP only during sleep
educated regarding exercise training for muscle stretching and an active range of motion
insulin at 0.83 IU/kg/day at discharge (degludec at 50 IU/day and aspart at 59 IU/day)
visited the outpatient clinic every 3 months
insulin (degludec at 76 IU/day and aspart at 60 IU/day)
continuing oxygen therapy with BiPAP during sleep
no elevation of the proBNP level
